steps.title: Simplify f(17)a
steps.content:
We need to find $f(17)a$, which is the product $f(17) \times a$.

First, evaluate $f(17)$ using the given function:
$$f(17) = \frac{|17|}{a} = \frac{17}{a}$$

Now multiply by $a$:
$$f(17)a = \frac{17}{a} \times a = 17$$

The absolute value in the numerator plays no role here since 17 is already positive.

steps.title: Evaluate f(8a) using the constraint on a
steps.content:
For $f(8a)$, the argument $8a$ is key. Since $a < 0$, we have $8a < 0$ (8 times a negative number is negative).

Because $8a$ is negative, its absolute value is:
$$|8a| = -(8a) = -8a$$

Therefore:
$$f(8a) = \frac{|8a|}{a} = \frac{-8a}{a} = -8$$

The negative value $a$ cancels with the negative sign from the absolute value, leaving a positive numerator divided by a negative denominator.
